title: lupinus sericeus var. flexuosus
intro: Lupinus sericeus var. flexuosus, also known as the silky lupine, is a variety of the lupine plant species that is native to the western United States and is characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and purple flowers. Traditionally, the silky lupine has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a diuretic to help reduce fluid retention. Herbal preparations of Lupinus sericeus var. flexuosus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it can also be used as a poultice to apply directly to the skin for wound healing and skin irritations.
